questions.prompt: Which of the following equations represents a hyperbola?
questions.explanation: A hyperbola is characterized by a subtraction (difference) between the squared terms in its standard form. The equation x²/a² - y²/b² = 1 represents a hyperbola opening horizontally, while y²/b² - x²/a² = 1 opens vertically. The other options represent: A) a circle (x² + y² = r²), C) a parabola (y² = 4px), and D) an ellipse (x²/a² + y²/b² = 1 where a ≠ b). The key distinguishing feature of a hyperbola is the minus sign between the two squared terms.
